<style>
</style>

FRAME

![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

EXTRUSION REFERENCE

![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

FRAME

| Qty | Hardware neede | Size | Tool’s needed | Qty | Parts needed |
| --- | -------------- | ---- | ------------- | --- | ------------ |
| 16  | M5X16 BHCS     | 3.0  | Hex driver    | 8   | A Extrusion  |

![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

PREPARE 8 EXTRUSIONS

The extrusions are going to be used in this
section. Prepare them as shown above, to make “A EXTRUSION SUB-ASSEMBLY”.  

Leave a gap between the head of the screw and the
extrusions, this will be needed in the following steps

FRAME

| Tool’s needed |            |     | Parts needed             |
| ------------- | ---------- | --- | ------------------------ |
| 3.0           | Hex driver | 4   | A Extrusion sub-assembly |
|               |            | 4   | B extrusions             |

![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

BUILD ON A FLAT SURFACE

Build the frame on a glass or granite surface
to ensure you can get it as square as possible. Using something like 123 blocks
or 90 degree squares may help.

Put the parts together, make sure to tighten
the M5x16 BHCS firmly, checking that it’s square.

FRAME

![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

It should look like this.

FRAME

|     | Tool’s needed |     | Parts needed             |
| --- | ------------- | --- | ------------------------ |
| 3.0 | Hex driver    |     | Frame                    |
|     |               | 4   | A Extrusion sub-assembly |

![](data:image/jpeg;base64,) 

![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

Double check that the frame is square.

FRAME

![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

| Qty | Hardware needed | Size | Tool’s needed | Qty | Parts needed    |
| --- | --------------- | ---- | ------------- | --- | --------------- |
| 4   | M5X16 BHCS      | 3.0  | Hex driver    | 2   | A Extrusion     |
|     |                 |      |               | 4   | Corner Brackets |

![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

This is the “Bed attachment extrusions”  

FRAME

|     | Hardware needed    | Tool’s needed |     |     | Parts needed              |
| --- | ------------------ | ------------- | --- | --- | ------------------------- |
| 4   | M5X10 BHCS         | Hex driver    | 3.0 | 2   | Bed attachment extrusions |
| 4   | M5 Precision Space |               |     |     |                           |

LOOK AT THE NEXT PAGE FOR
PLACEMENT AND POSITION OF LOCATION!

![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)  
*The brass M5 Precision Spacer are used in
place of the M5 Shim. This will be for all M5 Shims in the guide unless noted

FRAME

![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

| POSITION BED EXTRUSIONS Find the centreline of the printer and position the bed extrusions as shown in the diagram to the left. The distance between the extrusions is 130mm centred on the centreline of the printer.   1/2 printer width for standard sizes: 250 spec 205mm 300 spec 230mm 350 spec 255mm | ALL UNITS ARE METRIC If a unit is not specified assume it’s metric. All distances are called out in millimeters. |
| ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |

FRAME

![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

CHECK FOR SQUARENESS

Verify the angle of all corners and the
overall squareness by measuring the diagonals. Refer to the second half of the
linked video for additional information.

https://voron.link/kdtpzam
